display.purpose: Page H1 only. Tight, dramatic serif set close (line-height 0.82). Emphasized words turn rust.

headline.purpose: Section H2s. The structural beats of a page.

title.purpose: Finding-card headlines and serif object titles; pull quotes use the same family italic.

body.purpose: Reading copy in Manrope. Measure capped at 65-75ch.

label.purpose: All metadata: tiers, sources, eyebrows, captions, nav. DM Mono, uppercase, letter-spaced.

shadows.name: signature-offset
shadows.purpose: The only shadow in the system. Hard, blur-less sage offset under hero/feature cards. Reads as print registration, not elevation. Tightens to 8px 8px 0 on small screens.

motion.name: state-fade
motion.purpose: Filter chips and finding visibility. Restrained state-change feedback only, no choreography.

components.name: Principle Card
components.description: The signature feature card: forest ground, lime mono labels, serif pull quote, and the one allowed hard offset shadow.
components.html:
01 / PRINCIPLE
“Avoid catastrophic, irreversible errors first.”

Bone, neurons, enamel, hearing, vision, and accumulated damage do not fully recover.

Evidence×Impact×Irreversibility
components.css: .ds-principle-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; background: #173b31; color: #fff; padding: 34px; min-height: 410px; box-shadow: 16px 16px 0 #a8bd9d; max-width: 340px; } .ds-card-index { font: 10px 'DM Mono', monospace; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: #d7ee75; } .ds-card-quote { font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-size: 36px; line-height: 1.05; letter-spacing: -0.025em; margin: 55px 0 22px; } .ds-card-body { color: #c7d2cc; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.7; margin: 0; } .ds-card-rule { margin-top: auto; padding-top: 24px; border-top: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.2); display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font: 10px 'DM Mono', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; color: #d7ee75; }

components.name: Tier Badge
components.description: Square mono badge encoding evidence certainty. Tier is never color-only: the label text always rides alongside.
components.html: T1 T2 T3
components.css: .ds-tier { display: inline-block; padding: 5px 8px; font: 10px 'DM Mono', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; } .ds-tier-1 { background: #173b31; color: #fff; } .ds-tier-2 { background: #b84d2e; color: #fff; } .ds-tier-info { background: #e9e4d8; color: #657069; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: The one rounded element in the system. Hairline pill at rest; fills forest on hover/active. Selection by fill, never a colored stripe.
components.css: .ds-filter { border: 1px solid rgba(27,37,33,0.18); background: transparent; border-radius: 100px; padding: 9px 16px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 11px; color: #657069; font-family: 'Manrope', sans-serif; transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-filter:hover, .ds-filter-active { color: #fff; border-color: #173b31; background: #173b31; } .ds-filter:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b84d2e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Primary Link
components.description: The default call to action: an underlined mono link with a trailing glyph, not a filled button.
components.css: .ds-primary-link { display: inline-flex; gap: 30px; align-items: center; text-decoration: none; color: #1b2521; border-bottom: 1px solid #1b2521; padding: 0 0 9px; font: 700 13px 'DM Mono', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; } .ds-primary-link:hover { color: #b84d2e; border-color: #b84d2e; } .ds-primary-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #b84d2e; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Finding Card
components.description: A catalogue cell inside the hairline findings grid: square, flat, serif title, mono lever tag, rust lead stat.
components.html:
T1Exercise

Move most days.

30%lower all-cause
mortality
components.css: .ds-finding { display: flex; flex-direction: column; min-height: 300px; padding: 30px; border: 1px solid rgba(27,37,33,0.18); background: rgba(255,255,255,0.12); } .ds-finding-top { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; align-items: center; } .ds-tier { padding: 5px 8px; font: 10px 'DM Mono', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-tier-1 { background: #173b31; color: #fff; } .ds-lever { font: 10px 'DM Mono', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; opacity: 0.65; color: #1b2521; } .ds-finding-title { font-family: 'Newsreader', Georgia, serif; font-size: 42px; line-height: 1; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: -0.03em; margin: 38px 0 12px; color: #1b2521; } .ds-stat { display: flex; align-items: flex-end; gap: 15px; margin-top: auto; padding-top: 28px; } .ds-stat strong { font: 500 43px 'Newsreader', serif; color: #b84d2e; } .ds-stat span { font-size: 10px; line-height: 1.35; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; opacity: 0.68; padding-bottom: 6px; color: #1b2521; }

components.name: Lever Row
components.description: The catalogue index row: mono columns separated by hairlines; completed rows darken, the active row highlights lime.
components.css: .ds-lever-list { border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(27,37,33,0.18); } .ds-lever-row { min-height: 76px; display: grid; grid-template-columns: 80px 1.5fr 1fr 100px; align-items: center; border-top: 1px solid rgba(27,37,33,0.18); color: #657069; } .ds-lever-row span, .ds-lever-row em, .ds-lever-row b { font: 10px 'DM Mono', monospace; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; } .ds-lever-row strong { font: 600 15px 'Manrope', sans-serif; } .ds-lever-row b { text-align: right; } .ds-complete { color: #1b2521; } .ds-complete b { color: #46715d; } .ds-active { background: #d7ee75; color: #1b2521; padding-inline: 18px; } .ds-active b { color: #b84d2e; }

narrative.overview: This is a reference printed to last, not an app and not a campaign. Picture a well-set almanac on warm uncoated paper: botanical greens, a terracotta accent used sparingly, serif headlines with editorial confidence, and small monospace marginalia that read like a careful researcher's annotations. The aesthetic carries the project's strategic line: trust is earned by showing the evidence and admitting uncertainty, so the design favors legible structure and quiet authority over flourish. Density is editorial, not dashboard: generous vertical rhythm, large serif display type, and a strict hairline grid organizing findings like entries in a catalogue. The one piece of theater allowed is a hard, blur-less offset shadow on a feature card, a printerly block of sage that reads like ink registration, never soft web-glow.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper ground (#f3f0e7), never pure white, never dark mode.
Serif display (Newsreader) for voice; monospace (DM Mono) for evidence marginalia.
Restrained palette: forest + paper carry the surface, rust accent appears on under ~10%.
Sharp and printerly: 0px radius, hairline rules, square tier badges.
Flat by default, with one signature hard offset shadow as the only allowed elevation.

rules.name: The Rare Rust Rule
rules.body: Rust is the only accent and it appears on roughly <=10% of any screen. Use it for one emphasized word, one lead stat, or one badge, never as a fill across a region. If two rust elements compete for attention in the same view, one of them is wrong.

rules.name: The No-White, No-Dark-Mode Rule
rules.body: The ground is always warm paper (#f3f0e7). Never #fff, never #000, and never a dark theme. Depth comes from paper to paper-deep to forest, in that order.

rules.name: The Mono Marginalia Rule
rules.body: Anything that is metadata, not prose, is set in DM Mono, uppercase, letter-spaced: tiers, sources, indices, captions, status. Prose is never mono; mono is never a paragraph.

rules.name: The Serif-Speaks Rule
rules.body: Headlines, titles, claims, and pull quotes are always serif (Newsreader). Sans (Manrope) is for reading body and UI only. Never set a headline in sans.

rules.name: The One Shadow Rule
rules.body: The hard sage offset block is the only shadow permitted, and only on hero/feature cards. No box-shadow with a blur radius greater than 0 may appear anywhere. If you reach for a soft drop-shadow to separate two surfaces, use a hairline border or a tonal step instead.

dos: Do keep the ground warm paper (#f3f0e7). Layer depth as paper to paper-deep (#e9e4d8) to forest (#173b31).
Do reserve rust (#b84d2e) for a single emphasis per view: one word, one stat, or one badge.
Do set all metadata (tiers, sources, captions, eyebrows, indices) in DM Mono, uppercase, letter-spaced.
Do set every headline, title, and pull quote in Newsreader serif.
Do separate cards with the 1px hairline grid and keep corners square (0 radius).
Do keep the one signature shadow (16px 16px 0 sage, no blur) for feature cards only.
Do label evidence tiers with text (T1-T4) alongside color, and cap reading measure at 65-75ch.
donts: Don't evoke supplement / biohacker hype: no neon, no gradients, no urgency, no 'optimize everything' energy.
Don't drift toward clinical / WebMD sterility: no teal-and-white medical palette, no pure-white backgrounds, no stock photography.
Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no hero-metric template, no identical icon-card grid, no gradient accents, no dark mode.
Don't produce a dense academic PDF: rigor must stay legible; never a wall of text with no hierarchy.
Don't use any box-shadow with a blur radius (the only shadow is the hard sage offset).
Don't use a colored border-left/border-right stripe to mark a card, alert, or selection; use a full hairline, a tonal fill, or a forest fill instead.
Don't encode evidence tier or ranking strength by color alone; always include the label.
Don't set lime text on paper (it fails contrast); lime is for use on forest only.
